payload.plan: verify tags.all/optional API (done: role-tags.test.ts confirms FlowHandle[] / handle-or-undefined), probe multi-binding order, then TASK.md + answer-key + check-t8.mjs + reference through 5 gates + transplant/fake adversarials
payload.task: T-8 severe-weather alert fan-out (Tier C, port multiplicity)

api_findings: FINDING 1 (feeds skill + eval): tags.all in FLOW deps collects via collectFromHierarchy (src/scope.ts:1226) — ONE binding per context-chain level (context data is a Map keyed by tag.key). Multiple same-tag bindings in a single createScope/createContext tags array collapse to one. Registration multiplicity at ONE scope is only expressible via ATOM deps, where tags.all uses tag.collect over the raw scope tags array (src/scope.ts:1081) and returns every binding in order. Verified by probe: flow-direct=['radio'], atom-registry=['radio','siren','sms'].

FINDING 2: a tag carrying an ARRAY of flows type-projects to FlowHandle[][] per Lite.Projected but FAILS at runtime (h.exec is not a function) — projectTagValue does not recurse arrays. Flow-valued tags also reject in atom deps ('Flow deps require an ExecutionContext'). Therefore the T-8 channel port carries a plain capability spec {name, send}, fanned out via named edges ctx.exec({fn,params,name}) — params is REQUIRED (undocumented in README snippet, which omits it).

CONSEQUENCE: T-8 keeps domain + concept (port multiplicity via tags.all, optional policy via tags.optional, per-channel isolation) but the canonical topology is registry-atom(tags.all) + flow(registry, tags.optional) + per-channel named fn-exec. dkr-3b T8-D1 wording ('channels resolved via tags.all' in feature flow deps) would NOT work against the real library — answer key documents the verified shape.
payload.deliverable: reference-solution + checker green (all 5 gates exit 0, 12/12 checks)
